shortDef.en: A label that identifies the format of a file or data stream, used in HTTP headers and HTML attributes.

definition.en: A MIME type (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions type, also called a media type or content type) is a standardised label that identifies the format of a file or data stream. It consists of a type and subtype separated by a slash: image/png, text/html, application/json, audio/mpeg. MIME types are used in HTTP Content-Type headers (telling the browser what kind of data it received), HTML attributes (type='image/webp' in a source element), file upload inputs (accept='image/*'), and email attachments (their original purpose). The IANA (Internet Assigned Numbers Authority) maintains the official registry of MIME types.

examples.en: image/jpeg — identifies a JPEG image file
application/json — identifies a JSON data payload in an API response
text/html; charset=utf-8 — identifies an HTML page with UTF-8 encoding
multipart/form-data — identifies a file upload form submission
